dimensions.name: provable-correctness
dimensions.gate: Contracts v0 and Capabilities v1 landing as CI-gated Arc 2 features rather than design documents
dimensions.metric: proof discharge on the verified kernel; fraction of a program's effects statically proven
dimensions.note: Lumen's correctness story is scoped contracts plus capability-effects, and both are design-stage today (neither landed). Wave W7 (build-order item 4) covers attaching proof alongside the ecosystem-lowering work; Arc 2 is where Contracts v0 and Capabilities v1 themselves land.

dimensions.name: numeric-exactness-decimal
dimensions.gate: 0.1d + 0.2d == 0.3 landing gated byte-identical across the interpreter and both native backends
dimensions.metric: byte-identical decimal literal arithmetic across interpreter and native backends
dimensions.value: bit-identical across interpreter, both native backends, and the self-hosted compiler; external Python-decimal oracle gate green
dimensions.note: Landed (D1-D5): Dec (1.50d, i64 scale 1e-6) is bit-identical across the interpreter, emit_fn.lm, emit_llvm.lm, and the self-hosted lumenc.lm, plus an independent external Python decimal.Decimal oracle over 200 seeded programs. Wall-clock D1-failing-first to D4-merge: 14h 14m 24s (docs/VELOCITY_LEDGER.md entry #1).

dimensions.name: determinism-and-replay
dimensions.gate: lumen run --record landing with a deterministic tape, gated in CI (Arc 2)
dimensions.metric: bit-identity across the interpreter and both native backends on every conformance program
dimensions.note: Determinism half is real and CI-gated today. Replay half (lumen run --record, deterministic tape) is Arc 2 work; ROADMAP_2036.md's gap register: 'none is implemented.'

dimensions.name: effect-safety-and-capability-sandboxing
dimensions.gate: the purity gate and a tenant-isolation type-error test becoming CI gates, per the capabilities RFC
dimensions.metric: provable no-effect fraction; multi-tenant isolation as a compile error
dimensions.note: Design-only today: only the Console capability exists (docs/rfcs/0001-capabilities-v1.md, status draft, PR #52). Koka still leads on effect-system expressiveness regardless. The live task tracker's wave W2 (Capabilities v1) targets this but is not yet named in either doc's own build-order text, so wave is left null here.

dimensions.name: toolchain-trust-self-hosting-fidelity
dimensions.metric: generation-2 compiler byte-identical to the frozen seed oracle
dimensions.note: Self-hosting is table stakes across the field; Lumen's differentiator is the bit-identity fixpoint gate itself, already CI-gated every commit, not a future milestone. No pending gate to flip it further.

dimensions.name: compiler-as-a-reward-environment
dimensions.gate: the RL-lift experiment running and publishing a measured authorship gain (Arc 3)
dimensions.metric: deterministic runs; stable E/W/C/R diagnostic code families; warm compile latency; promptgreen rig
dimensions.value: 0.5 ms cold / 1.5 ms incremental compile on 1,200 LOC (bench/DASHBOARD.md Pillar B)
dimensions.note: Won on today's grounded ingredients (deterministic runs, stable diagnostic codes, sub-millisecond warm compile, the hermetic promptgreen rig v0). The RL-lift experiment itself has not run.

dimensions.name: generated-code-speed-runtime
dimensions.gate: a native optimizer near LLVM/GCC parity (autovectorization, LTO, a numeric stdlib) landing
dimensions.metric: native artifact throughput vs hand-written C
dimensions.note: Lumen loses to the whole LLVM family and the JVM on real workloads today. Full Float coverage on the LLVM path is gated bit-identical (native/llvm_float_test.mjs, PR #54); that closes a coverage gap, it does not change the runtime-speed verdict.

dimensions.name: compile-latency-sub-axis
dimensions.gate: a cross-language compile-latency comparison against the field being designed and run in this tree
dimensions.metric: cold/incremental compile time; compiler throughput in kLOC/sec
dimensions.value: 0.5 ms cold / 1.5 ms incremental / 794.1 kLOC/sec vs a >100 kLOC/sec target (bench/DASHBOARD.md Pillar B)
dimensions.note: Lumen-only numbers today; no cross-language comparison has been designed or run in this tree. VISION_2036.md does not separately score compile latency against Python (its row 7 covers generated-code runtime speed only), hence python_verdict is null here rather than borrowed from 7a.

dimensions.name: build-determinism-and-supply-chain
dimensions.gate: the full air-gap test passing (Arc 1's exit gate)
dimensions.metric: no network during compile; single self-contained binary
dimensions.note: Tied with Go and Zig, not sole champion, until the air-gap test (Arc 1 exit gate) is green. The purity gate (tools/purity_gate.mjs) is an advisory reporter, not a blocking CI gate; node and clang still ride the development harnesses.

dimensions.name: ai-authorability-intent-to-green
dimensions.gate: the reinforcement-against-the-compiler loop producing a published Lumen-vs-Python authorship number
dimensions.note: Arc 3's kill criterion applies here: if reinforcement against the compiler shows no measurable authorship gain by end-2029, the plan pivots to the verified-intermediate-representation bet instead.

dimensions.name: governance-and-evolution-velocity
dimensions.value: 14h 14m 24s (Dec: D1 failing-first commit to D4 self-hosted-compiler-parity merge, docs/VELOCITY_LEDGER.md entry #1)
dimensions.note: Caveat: incumbents carry vast installed bases and their caution is a feature; Lumen is young and small, so speed is cheap today. The durable edge is the mechanism (an executable oracle, not a committee), not the current pace.

dimensions.name: debuggability
dimensions.gate: deterministic time-travel replay (the same Arc 2 tape as dimension 3's replay half) landing and being CI-gated
dimensions.metric: fix-application-rate (live); replay coverage of recorded runs (none exist yet)
dimensions.note: Kept as one entry rather than split into 11a/11b: the doc gives this row a single verdict word ('Split') rather than two separately-graded sub-verdicts the way it does for dimension 7. Live today: structured diagnostics with stable code families, plus compiler-applied confident fixes (lumen fix). Designed but not built: deterministic time-travel replay (same Arc 2 tape as dimension 3's replay half; ROADMAP_2036.md gap register: 'none is implemented'). Lost on tooling maturity: JVM, C++, and Python debuggers and profilers are decades ahead. Separately flagged in the PR report: VISION_2036.md's own row 11 still bundles the undelivered replay claim into a flat 'Won by design' without this caveat; the PR-A reconciliation fixed LANGUAGE_COMPARISON.md's row 11 but that fix did not propagate to VISION_2036.md.

dimensions.name: ecosystem-breadth-and-hiring
dimensions.metric: fraction of verified cores that lower and run on a host ecosystem
dimensions.note: Subsumption strategy, not a race to win the axis directly: lower verified cores to a host (C, LLVM, WASM, Python, Rust) so incumbent ecosystems become Lumen's output targets. The doc's own scoring rule defines 'lost today, must earn' as 'it either subsumes it or earns it,' so this is that same canonical verdict, not a separate category. Wave W7 covers the lowering-to-host work now; Arc 4's lower-to-any-runtime item is the larger later version.

dimensions.name: human-familiarity-today
dimensions.gate: the same reinforcement-against-the-compiler loop as dimension 9 producing a measurable gain
dimensions.metric: first-try-compile-rate for models that loaded the spec bundle
dimensions.note: Tied to the same corpus-and-reinforcement loop as dimension 9 (the doc says so directly). Tiny today; grown only by the corpus.
